relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Isaac blessed Jacob again and warned him not to take a woman from Canaan.
Jacob was sent toward Rebekah’s family, toward Laban’s daughters.
El Shaddai’s fruitfulness was spoken over him as an assembly of peoples.
The blessing of Abraham passed to Jacob and his seed in the land of sojourning.
Isaac sent Jacob away toward Paddan-Aram and Laban.
Esau saw that Jacob had been blessed and sent away for a family marriage.
He saw Jacob listening to father and mother.
Esau saw that Canaanite daughters were painful in Isaac’s eyes.
He went to Ishmael’s line, trying to repair the wound through another marriage.
Jacob left Beersheba and went toward Haran.
Night found him at a place, with stone for his head and uncertainty around him.
He dreamed of a stairway joining earth and heavens, with messengers ascending and descending.
The Eternal stood above or beside him and spoke the land and seed promise.
Dust-like seed and blessing for all families of the ground were promised through him.
The Eternal promised presence, keeping, return, and completion.
Jacob awoke to a hidden truth: the Eternal was in this place, and he had not known it.
Fear became awe. The place was house of the Divine and gate of the heavens.
Jacob raised the stone as a pillar and anointed it with oil.
He named the place Bethel, House of the Divine.
Jacob vowed around presence, protection, bread, clothing, and the way home.
If he returned in peace, the Eternal would be his Divine.
The stone would become sacred house, and Jacob promised a tenth from what he received.
